title: The Psychology of Human Development
subtitle: A Comprehensive Introduction to Developmental Psychology
author: Robert S. Siegler, with Judith Smetana, Nancy Eisenberg, and others
themes: Development occurs across the entire lifespan, not just in childhood
Nature and nurture interact at every stage of development
Cognitive development proceeds through stages but also continuous improvement
Social and emotional development is shaped by relationships, starting with attachment
Culture and context shape developmental trajectories
Each period of life has its own developmental tasks and challenges
excerpt: Human development is the study of change and continuity across the lifespan. From the moment of conception to the end of life, we are continually growing, adapting, and transforming. This book provides a comprehensive overview of the major theories, research findings, and applications of developmental psychology — the science of how and why people change over time.
